arts.ttl: Borussia Dortmund gathering pace ahead of Barcelona and Bayern Munich clashes
arts.sum: Thanks in part to Carney Chukwuemeka's debut goal, the Black-Yellows go into their season-defining matches in Barcelona and Munich as a team in form. It means that Dortmund's first two goals were created and finished by their young guns, with Adeyemi and Beier both being born in 2002 and Chukwuemeka in 2003.

arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer news: Mikel Arteta prepares €150m offer for Lautaro Martinez
arts.sum: Arsenal are prepared to pay €150 million to sign Inter Milan forward Lautaro Martinez this summer, according to a bombshell report in Spain. The Argentina international has scored 18 goals in 40 appearances across all competitions so far this season and Arsenal have rekindled their interest in him following rumours of a move to the Emirates Stadium on multiple occasions over the past few years.

arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: The aftermath of Bayern Munich vs. Inter Milan; Bayern making strong push for Real Madrid target Dean Huijsen?; Timo Werner, Antoine Griezmann to MLS?; Liverpool prepping for summer splurge; and MORE!
arts.sum: And while Bayern Munich are understood to be willing to offer the 19-year-old a lucrative five-year deal, Real Madrid remain the biggest threat to any side keen on the twice-capped Spain defender this summer as they are his boyhood club and he has openly admitted that he dreams of playing for them one day. The race to sign Bournemouth defender Dean Huijsen has taken a dramatic turn after Bayern Munich entered the race for his signing, and sources have informed us that Vincent Kompany is ready to go the extra mile to ensure they beat the likes of Liverpool and Real Madrid to his signature.

arts.ttl: “Life of a striker” — Harry Kane on his big miss in Bayern Munich’s Champions League loss to Inter Milan
arts.sum: Still, it looked a sure goal, the type of chance a striker of Kane’s quality is expected to bury, in the sort of moment a Champions League winning side is expected to conjure. Bayern Munich lost for the first time in 23 Champions League home games on Tuesday against Inter Milan .

arts.ttl: 'Important' Inter win nothing without second leg
arts.sum: Inter Milan's last-gasp 2-1 victory at Bayern Munich in the first leg of their Champions League quarte-final on Tuesday was the result of the Italian team's strong mentality but more work awaits, said Inter coach Simone Inzaghi.

arts.ttl: Inter's Martínez and Thuram could be Inzaghi's X factors in the Champions League
arts.sum: The performances from Inter's Lautaro Martínez and Marcus Thuram could be the deciding factor on how well the Nerazzurri do in the UEFA Champions League.

arts.ttl: Three Observations from Bayern Munich’s 2-1 Champions League collapse at home to Inter Milan
arts.sum: Bayern Munich dominated Inter Milan in the first leg of this Champions League tie but somehow ended up losing 2-1. Looking for more thoughts and analysis on Bayern Munich disappointing performance during a 2-1 loss to Inter Milan in the Champions League?

arts.ttl: Bavarian Podcast Works — Postgame Show: Bayern Munich 1-2 Inter Milan (Champions League)
arts.sum: Inter Milan was even good enough to hold off the bit of #MüllerMagic that Bayern Munich received in the second half. Bayern Munich hosted Inter Milan at the Allianz Arena for a Champions League showdown that just about everyone knew would be tough.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich 1-2 Inter Milan: Davide Frattesi hits late winner to silence Bavaria
arts.sum: The hosts enjoyed most of the ball but were twice hit against the run of play

arts.ttl: Kompany: Bayern ‘dominated’ for 30 minutes against Inter, this gives us ‘hope’ for return leg
arts.sum: Vincent Kompany expressed his frustration following his side’s 2-1 defeat to Inter in the first...
